title: Why do two embedding models give different results for the same text?
prompt:
Why do two embedding models give different results for the same text?

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
Different training data, architectures, dimensions, and training objectives produce fundamentally different vector spaces. A model trained on scientific papers clusters technical content differently than one trained on social media. A 384-dimension model (MiniLM) compresses meaning differently than a 1536-dimension model (OpenAI ada-002).

**Takeaway:** Never assume one embedding model is best. Benchmark on your domain. Plan for model changes from the start.
hint_notes: Never assume one embedding model is best. Benchmark on your domain. Plan for model changes from the start.
